sql_snippets.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) as transfer_count, ROUND(SUM(fee_revenue)::numeric, 2) as total_fee_revenue, ROUND(SUM(fx_margin_revenue)::numeric, 2) as total_fx_margin_revenue, ROUND(SUM(total_revenue)::numeric, 2) as total_revenue, MIN(created_at::date) as earliest_transfer, MAX(created_at::date) as latest_transfer FROM fct_transfers WHERE send_currency = 'GBP' AND is_completed = true AND created_at >= '2026-01-01' AND created_at < '2026-04-01'

SELECT status_clean, COUNT(*) as transfer_count, ROUND(SUM(total_revenue)::numeric, 2) as total_revenue FROM fct_transfers WHERE send_currency = 'GBP' AND created_at >= '2026-01-01' AND created_at < '2026-04-01' GROUP BY status_clean ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at)::date as revenue_month, TO_CHAR(DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at), 'Mon YYYY') as month_label, COUNT(*) as transfer_count, ROUND(SUM(fee_revenue)::numeric, 2) as fee_revenue, ROUND(SUM(fx_margin_revenue)::numeric, 2) as fx_margin_revenue, ROUND(SUM(total_revenue)::numeric, 2) as total_revenue FROM fct_transfers WHERE send_currency = 'GBP' AND is_completed = true AND created_at >= '2026-01-01' AND created_at < '2026-04-01' GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY 1

SELECT ROUND(SUM(fee_revenue)::numeric, 2) as fee_revenue, ROUND(SUM(fx_margin_revenue)::numeric, 2) as fx_margin_revenue, ROUND(SUM(total_revenue)::numeric, 2) as total_revenue, ROUND((SUM(fee_revenue) + SUM(fx_margin_revenue) - SUM(total_revenue))::numeric, 2) as promo_discount_implied FROM fct_transfers WHERE send_currency = 'GBP' AND is_completed = true AND created_at >= '2026-01-01' AND created_at < '2026-04-01'

SELECT COUNT(*) as transfer_count, ROUND(SUM(fee_revenue)::numeric, 2) as total_fee_revenue, ROUND(SUM(fx_margin_revenue)::numeric, 2) as total_fx_margin_revenue, ROUND(SUM(total_revenue)::numeric, 2) as total_revenue, MIN(created_at::date) as earliest_transfer, MAX(created_at::date) as latest_transfer FROM fct_transfers WHERE send_currency = 'GBP' AND is_completed = true AND created_at >= '2026-01-01' AND created_at < '2026-04-01'

SELECT status_clean, COUNT(*) as transfer_count, ROUND(SUM(total_revenue)::numeric, 2) as total_revenue FROM fct_transfers WHERE send_currency = 'GBP' AND created_at >= '2026-01-01' AND created_at < '2026-04-01' GROUP BY status_clean ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at)::date as revenue_month, TO_CHAR(DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at), 'Mon YYYY') as month_label, COUNT(*) as transfer_count, ROUND(SUM(fee_revenue)::numeric, 2) as fee_revenue, ROUND(SUM(fx_margin_revenue)::numeric, 2) as fx_margin_revenue, ROUND(SUM(total_revenue)::numeric, 2) as total_revenue FROM fct_transfers WHERE send_currency = 'GBP' AND is_completed = true AND created_at >= '2026-01-01' AND created_at < '2026-04-01' GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY 1

SELECT ROUND(SUM(fee_revenue)::numeric, 2) as fee_revenue, ROUND(SUM(fx_margin_revenue)::numeric, 2) as fx_margin_revenue, ROUND(SUM(total_revenue)::numeric, 2) as total_revenue, ROUND((SUM(fee_revenue) + SUM(fx_margin_revenue) - SUM(total_revenue))::numeric, 2) as promo_discount_implied FROM fct_transfers WHERE send_currency = 'GBP' AND is_completed = true AND created_at >= '2026-01-01' AND created_at < '2026-04-01'

SELECT COUNT(*) as transfer_count, ROUND(SUM(fee_revenue)::numeric, 2) as total_fee_revenue, ROUND(SUM(fx_margin_revenue)::numeric, 2) as total_fx_margin_revenue, ROUND(SUM(total_revenue)::numeric, 2) as total_revenue, MIN(created_at::date) as earliest_transfer, MAX(created_at::date) as latest_transfer FROM analytics_marts.fct_transfers WHERE send_currency = 'GBP' AND is_completed = true AND created_at >= '2026-01-01' AND created_at < '2026-04-01'

SELECT status_clean, COUNT(*) as transfer_count, ROUND(SUM(total_revenue)::numeric, 2) as total_revenue FROM analytics_marts.fct_transfers WHERE send_currency = 'GBP' AND created_at >= '2026-01-01' AND created_at < '2026-04-01' GROUP BY status_clean ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at)::date as revenue_month, TO_CHAR(DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at), 'Mon YYYY') as month_label, COUNT(*) as transfer_count, ROUND(SUM(fee_revenue)::numeric, 2) as fee_revenue, ROUND(SUM(fx_margin_revenue)::numeric, 2) as fx_margin_revenue, ROUND(SUM(total_revenue)::numeric, 2) as total_revenue FROM analytics_marts.fct_transfers WHERE send_currency = 'GBP' AND is_completed = true AND created_at >= '2026-01-01' AND created_at < '2026-04-01' GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY 1

SELECT ROUND(SUM(fee_revenue)::numeric, 2) as fee_revenue, ROUND(SUM(fx_margin_revenue)::numeric, 2) as fx_margin_revenue, ROUND(SUM(total_revenue)::numeric, 2) as total_revenue, ROUND((SUM(fee_revenue) + SUM(fx_margin_revenue) - SUM(total_revenue))::numeric, 2) as promo_discount_implied FROM analytics_marts.fct_transfers WHERE send_currency = 'GBP' AND is_completed = true AND created_at >= '2026-01-01' AND created_at < '2026-04-01'

stages.label: Boot/tool setup and prompt intake
stages.note: Initial Slack request, assistant status text, notebook tool discovery.

stages.label: dbt project scouting and planning
stages.note: Explore subagent, Glob/Read/Grep over dbt files, and long planning before notebook edits.

stages.label: Notebook rebuild after edit transaction failure
stages.note: First edit partially applied because a stale cell id was missing; agent inspected notebook and rebuilt cells.

stages.label: Execution/debug loop: SDK init, connection, schema prefix
stages.note: Fixed missing sp.init(), wrong connection name nala, and unqualified fct_transfers table references.

stages.label: Successful notebook execution and summary update
stages.note: Ran notebook successfully against analytics_marts.fct_transfers and updated summary/result cells.

stages.label: Final answer generation
stages.note: Generated final JSON result, methodology, caveats, and chart metadata.

text_snapshots.content: I'm starting the analysis for your Q1 2026 GBP transfer revenue question. Let me first load the tools I need and scout your dbt project and database schema.
Good, tools are loaded. Let me now scout the dbt project structure and read the skill files to understand the notebook SDK, while also checking the current notebook state.
Now let me check the current notebook state and scout the dbt project in parallel to plan my queries.
The edit reported a transaction error but individual operations showed "ok". Let me check the actual notebook state to verify what was applied.
The transaction partially applied — only Hbol remains (MJUe and vblA were deleted but adds were rolled back). Let me rebuild the notebook by adding all needed cells in a fresh edit.
All 13 cells added successfully. Now running all cells to execute the queries and generate charts.
The setup cell failed because `sp.init()` wasn't called. I need to initialize the SDK first. Let me fix the setup cell.
Good, cell updated. Running all cells again to pick up the SDK initialization.
The SDK initialized, but the connection name "nala" wasn't found. Let me discover the available connections first.
Found it — the connection is named "fin-db" (matching the user's "fin db"). Let me update the setup cell and run the full notebook.
Now running all cells with the correct connection.

The connection works but tables need schema prefixes. I can see `analytics_intermediate.*` tables, so the marts are likely in `analytics_marts`. Let me fix all query cells to use `analytics_marts.fct_transfers`.
All query cells updated with `analytics_marts.fct_transfers`. Running the full notebook now.

All cells executed successfully with clean data. Here's what I found:
- **£2,530.33** total completed GBP transfer revenue in Q1 2026
- **190 transfers** across all 3 months
- Revenue is 91.6% FX margin, 7.7% fees
- Monthly reconciliation ✅ passes

Now let me update the executive summary and finding headings with actual results.
Summary cells updated. Running final verification to confirm all cells execute cleanly.

All 14 cells executed cleanly — zero errors, all reconciliation checks pass, both charts rendered. The notebook is complete. Here is the final analysis result:

```json
{
  "summary": "Q1 2026 completed GBP transfer revenue: £2,530.33 across 190 transfers (91.6% FX margin, 7.7% fees)",
  "confidenceScore": 0.90,
  "finalAnswer": "## Executive Summary and Explorations

- **Q1 2026 completed GBP transfer revenue: £2,530.33** across 190 completed transfers (Jan 1 – Mar 31, 2026).
- Revenue is heavily FX-margin-driven: £2,316.94 FX margin (91.6%) vs £195.57 fees (7.7%), with a small £17.82 net promo credit.
- Monthly trajectory: Jan £876.43 (69 transfers) → Feb £867.78 (43 transfers) → Mar £786.12 (78 transfers). Broadly flat with a slight downward trend, though transfer volume increased in March.
- Only completed transfers are included. Including all statuses (pending, failed, cancelled, refunded) would give £2,793.60 across 222 transfers.
- **Assumptions:** "UK (GBP) customers" interpreted as `send_currency = 'GBP'`; only `is_completed = true` transfers included; revenue dated by `created_at` not settlement date.
- **Exclusions:** Non-GBP transfers from UK-domiciled customers; pending/failed/cancelled/refunded transfers.
- **Known gaps:** FX margin revenue is modeled (`send_amount × fx_margin_bps / 10,000`), not actual realized execution margin.
- **Sensitivity:** If the business definition of "UK customers" includes non-GBP senders domiciled in UK, or if revenue recognition should use completion date, the figure could shift.
- Steps run: SDK init → governed connection to `fin-db` → total revenue query → status breakdown validation → monthly trend query → monthly chart → monthly reconciliation → component breakdown query → component chart → component reconciliation.

## Detailed Research

### Total Q1 2026 Completed GBP Transfer Revenue
Queried `analytics_marts.fct_transfers` with filters `send_currency = 'GBP'`, `is_completed = true`, and `created_at` in [2026-01-01, 2026-04-01). Result: 190 transfers, £195.57 fee revenue, £2,316.94 FX margin revenue, £2,530.33 total revenue. Date range spans Jan 1 to Mar 31.

### Status Breakdown Validation
All GBP transfers in Q1 (regardless of status): completed 190 (£2,530.33), refunded 5 (£88.82), pending 14 (£79.24), failed 7 (£51.38), cancelled 6 (£43.83). Total across all statuses: £2,793.60 from 222 transfers. Completed transfers represent 85.6% of all Q1 GBP transfers by count and 90.6% by revenue.

### Monthly Revenue Trend
- January: 69 transfers, £56.69 fees, £815.78 FX margin, **£876.43 total**
- February: 43 transfers, £55.78 fees, £808.04 FX margin, **£867.78 total**
- March: 78 transfers, £83.10 fees, £693.12 FX margin, **£786.12 total**

Monthly sum (£2,530.33) reconciles exactly to the total query. Revenue dipped ~10% from Jan to Mar despite March having the highest transfer count, driven by lower FX margin revenue per transfer.

### Revenue Component Breakdown
- Fee revenue: £195.57 (7.7% of total)
- FX margin revenue: £2,316.94 (91.6% of total)
- Implied promo credit: £17.82 (fee + FX margin = £2,512.51, but total is £2,530.33, indicating net promo credits added to revenue)
- Components reconcile: fee + FX margin – implied promo = total ✅

The revenue mix is overwhelmingly FX margin. Transfer fees are a minor contributor. The small net promo credit suggests promotional bonus adjustments in Q1.

## Confidence Score: 0.90

- **Source:** Canonical `analytics_marts.fct_transfers` dbt mart table, queried via governed `fin-db` connection through the SignalPilot data SDK.
- **Filters applied:** `send_currency = 'GBP'`, `is_completed = true`, `created_at >= '2026-01-01' AND created_at < '2026-04-01'`.
- **Validation checks passed:** Monthly sums reconcile to total (£2,530.33 ✅). Component breakdown reconciles (fee + FX margin – promo = total ✅). Full Q1 date range confirmed (Jan 1 – Mar 31). All 3 months represented with non-zero data.
- **What could raise confidence:** Cross-checking against `fct_revenue_daily` mart; confirming FX margin against actual execution data; validating that `send_currency = 'GBP'` matches the intended UK customer definition.
- **What could lower confidence:** If "UK customers" should include non-GBP senders domiciled in the UK; if revenue recognition should use completion/settlement date rather than creation date; if FX margin model diverges materially from realized margins.",
  "gotchas": [
    "Only completed transfers included (is_completed = true). Pending, failed, cancelled, and refunded transfers (£263.27 additional) are excluded.",
    "UK (GBP) customers interpreted as send_currency = 'GBP'. UK-domiciled customers sending in other currencies are not captured.",
    "Revenue dated by transfer creation date (created_at), not settlement or completion date.",
    "FX margin revenue is modeled (send_amount × fx_margin_bps / 10,000), not actual realized FX execution margin.",
    "A net promo credit of £17.82 means total_revenue exceeds fee + FX margin components — promo/bonus adjustments are embedded in the total."
  ],
  "analysisMethod": "Queried the dbt analytics_marts.fct_transfers mart via governed fin-db connection using the SignalPilot notebook SDK (sp.init → sp.connect → db.query). Ran four governed SQL queries: total Q1 GBP completed revenue, status breakdown for all Q1 GBP transfers, monthly completed revenue breakdown, and component split (fee vs FX margin). Validated with monthly-to-total reconciliation (exact match) and component-to-total reconciliation (exact match after promo adjustment). Two charts generated: monthly revenue bar chart and component bar/pie chart. All evidence, calculations, and checks executed in notebook cells — no MCP-only or hardcoded results.",
  "notionComment": "• Q1 2026 completed GBP transfer revenue: **£2,530.33** from 190 transfers (Jan–Mar 2026)
• Revenue mix: £2,316.94 FX margin (91.6%) + £195.57 fees (7.7%) + £17.82 net promo credit
• Monthly trend: Jan £876 → Feb £868 → Mar £786 — broadly flat, slight dip in March despite higher transfer volume
• Only completed transfers included; all statuses would total £2,793.60 across 222 transfers
• Revenue uses transfer creation date and modeled FX margin — see notebook for full caveats and methodology",
  "notionCharts": [
    {
      "title": "Q1 2026 GBP Monthly Revenue",
      "url": "notebooks/slack/q1_gbp_monthly_revenue.png",
      "caption": "Monthly completed GBP transfer revenue trended slightly downward from £876 in January to £786 in March, despite higher transfer volume in March.",
      "altText": "Bar chart showing Q1 2026 GBP transfer revenue by month: Jan £876, Feb £868, Mar £786",
      "includeInComment": true,
      "includeOnPage": true
    },
    {
      "title": "Q1 2026 GBP Revenue Components",
      "url": "notebooks/slack/q1_gbp_revenue_components.png",
      "caption": "FX margin dominates the revenue mix at 91.6%, with transfer fees contributing just 7.7% of the £2,530.33 total.",
      "altText": "Side-by-side bar and pie chart showing revenue split: £2,317 FX margin (91.6%) vs £196 fees (7.7%)",
      "includeInComment": false,
      "includeOnPage": true
    }
  ]
}
```

final_result.summary: Q1 2026 completed GBP transfer revenue: £2,530.33 across 190 transfers (91.6% FX margin, 7.7% fees)
final_result.final_answer:
## Executive Summary and Explorations

- **Q1 2026 completed GBP transfer revenue: £2,530.33** across 190 completed transfers (Jan 1 – Mar 31, 2026).
- Revenue is heavily FX-margin-driven: £2,316.94 FX margin (91.6%) vs £195.57 fees (7.7%), with a small £17.82 net promo credit.
- Monthly trajectory: Jan £876.43 (69 transfers) → Feb £867.78 (43 transfers) → Mar £786.12 (78 transfers). Broadly flat with a slight downward trend, though transfer volume increased in March.
- Only completed transfers are included. Including all statuses (pending, failed, cancelled, refunded) would give £2,793.60 across 222 transfers.
- **Assumptions:** "UK (GBP) customers" interpreted as `send_currency = 'GBP'`; only `is_completed = true` transfers included; revenue dated by `created_at` not settlement date.
- **Exclusions:** Non-GBP transfers from UK-domiciled customers; pending/failed/cancelled/refunded transfers.
- **Known gaps:** FX margin revenue is modeled (`send_amount × fx_margin_bps / 10,000`), not actual realized execution margin.
- **Sensitivity:** If the business definition of "UK customers" includes non-GBP senders domiciled in UK, or if revenue recognition should use completion date, the figure could shift.
- Steps run: SDK init → governed connection to `fin-db` → total revenue query → status breakdown validation → monthly trend query → monthly chart → monthly reconciliation → component breakdown query → component chart → component reconciliation.

## Detailed Research

### Total Q1 2026 Completed GBP Transfer Revenue
Queried `analytics_marts.fct_transfers` with filters `send_currency = 'GBP'`, `is_completed = true`, and `created_at` in [2026-01-01, 2026-04-01). Result: 190 transfers, £195.57 fee revenue, £2,316.94 FX margin revenue, £2,530.33 total revenue. Date range spans Jan 1 to Mar 31.

### Status Breakdown Validation
All GBP transfers in Q1 (regardless of status): completed 190 (£2,530.33), refunded 5 (£88.82), pending 14 (£79.24), failed 7 (£51.38), cancelled 6 (£43.83). Total across all statuses: £2,793.60 from 222 transfers. Completed transfers represent 85.6% of all Q1 GBP transfers by count and 90.6% by revenue.

### Monthly Revenue Trend
- January: 69 transfers, £56.69 fees, £815.78 FX margin, **£876.43 total**
- February: 43 transfers, £55.78 fees, £808.04 FX margin, **£867.78 total**
- March: 78 transfers, £83.10 fees, £693.12 FX margin, **£786.12 total**

Monthly sum (£2,530.33) reconciles exactly to the total query. Revenue dipped ~10% from Jan to Mar despite March having the highest transfer count, driven by lower FX margin revenue per transfer.

### Revenue Component Breakdown
- Fee revenue: £195.57 (7.7% of total)
- FX margin revenue: £2,316.94 (91.6% of total)
- Implied promo credit: £17.82 (fee + FX margin = £2,512.51, but total is £2,530.33, indicating net promo credits added to revenue)
- Components reconcile: fee + FX margin – implied promo = total ✅

The revenue mix is overwhelmingly FX margin. Transfer fees are a minor contributor. The small net promo credit suggests promotional bonus adjustments in Q1.

## Confidence Score: 0.90

- **Source:** Canonical `analytics_marts.fct_transfers` dbt mart table, queried via governed `fin-db` connection through the SignalPilot data SDK.
- **Filters applied:** `send_currency = 'GBP'`, `is_completed = true`, `created_at >= '2026-01-01' AND created_at < '2026-04-01'`.
- **Validation checks passed:** Monthly sums reconcile to total (£2,530.33 ✅). Component breakdown reconciles (fee + FX margin – promo = total ✅). Full Q1 date range confirmed (Jan 1 – Mar 31). All 3 months represented with non-zero data.
- **What could raise confidence:** Cross-checking against `fct_revenue_daily` mart; confirming FX margin against actual execution data; validating that `send_currency = 'GBP'` matches the intended UK customer definition.
- **What could lower confidence:** If "UK customers" should include non-GBP senders domiciled in the UK; if revenue recognition should use completion/settlement date rather than creation date; if FX margin model diverges materially from realized margins.
gotchas: Only completed transfers included (is_completed = true). Pending, failed, cancelled, and refunded transfers (£263.27 additional) are excluded.
UK (GBP) customers interpreted as send_currency = 'GBP'. UK-domiciled customers sending in other currencies are not captured.
Revenue dated by transfer creation date (created_at), not settlement or completion date.
FX margin revenue is modeled (send_amount × fx_margin_bps / 10,000), not actual realized FX execution margin.
A net promo credit of £17.82 means total_revenue exceeds fee + FX margin components — promo/bonus adjustments are embedded in the total.
final_result.analysis_method: Queried the dbt analytics_marts.fct_transfers mart via governed fin-db connection using the SignalPilot notebook SDK (sp.init → sp.connect → db.query). Ran four governed SQL queries: total Q1 GBP completed revenue, status breakdown for all Q1 GBP transfers, monthly completed revenue breakdown, and component split (fee vs FX margin). Validated with monthly-to-total reconciliation (exact match) and component-to-total reconciliation (exact match after promo adjustment). Two charts generated: monthly revenue bar chart and component bar/pie chart. All evidence, calculations, and checks executed in notebook cells — no MCP-only or hardcoded results.
final_result.notion_comment:
• Q1 2026 completed GBP transfer revenue: **£2,530.33** from 190 transfers (Jan–Mar 2026)
• Revenue mix: £2,316.94 FX margin (91.6%) + £195.57 fees (7.7%) + £17.82 net promo credit
• Monthly trend: Jan £876 → Feb £868 → Mar £786 — broadly flat, slight dip in March despite higher transfer volume
• Only completed transfers included; all statuses would total £2,793.60 across 222 transfers
• Revenue uses transfer creation date and modeled FX margin — see notebook for full caveats and methodology

facts.final_summary: Q1 2026 completed GBP transfer revenue: £2,530.33 across 190 transfers (91.6% FX margin, 7.7% fees)
